issues.title: nn/4 supports CUDA scoring, not declarative DILP training
issues.expected_behavior: XLOG should let a project declare neural predicates, learned clauses, symbolic rule weights, and proof-level training objectives in one executable neuro-symbolic program.
issues.actual_behavior: The v0.8.9 closure was a scalar-bias surrogate (regex parsing, no engine evaluation) and was re-resolved post-v0.9.2: train_neurosymbolic_program now desugars trainable_rule into guard neural predicates, compiles the whole source through the native xlog parser (pyxlog.Program.compile), and trains via CompiledProgram.forward_backward so the rule body is evaluated by the compiled GPU circuit and gradients flow through actual inference into both networks and rule weights. Credit assignment is exported through DifferentiableProofTraceMap.
issues.proposed_fix: Route the symbolic part through the real engine: native parse of the whole program, rule weights as guard-predicate probabilities inside the compiled circuit, joint gradients from circuit evaluation, behavioral (mutation + exact-semantics) regression assertions.

suggested_regression_test.assertion: Engine-reported query probabilities equal exact circuit conjunction semantics with frozen parameters; mutating only the trainable rule body changes the loss; invalid xlog is rejected by the real parser; proof-level credit assignment flows through DifferentiableProofTraceMap.

issues.title: Proof paths can be enumerated but not differentiated
issues.expected_behavior: The compiler/runtime should expose proof traces as differentiable objects with clause IDs, support tensors, weights, and gradients.
issues.actual_behavior: Resolved in v0.8.9 by xlog_logic::DifferentiableProofTraceMap, which exports stable proof IDs, clause IDs, support atoms, symbolic weights, logistic loss, and nonzero clause gradients.
issues.proposed_fix: Implemented proof-trace export and differentiable proof aggregation APIs that preserve clause IDs and expose gradient hooks for pyxlog and CUDA training.

suggested_regression_test.assertion: A proof_path query returns stable proof IDs and a differentiable trace map whose clause weights receive nonzero gradients during training.

issues.title: Learned rule inventories are not produced by pyxlog for UCR-style transfer
issues.expected_behavior: pyxlog should produce an auditable learned-rule inventory with selected clauses, rejected clauses, scores, training folds, and held-out safety metadata.
issues.actual_behavior: Resolved in v0.8.9 by pyxlog.ilp.inventory.build_rule_inventory and train_and_promote(...).rule_inventory, including selected and rejected clauses, scores, training fold, held-out exclusions, and base-kernel checksum status.
issues.proposed_fix: Implemented stable pyxlog rule-inventory JSON including fold membership, excluded held-out domains, selected clauses, clause weights, and BFO-kernel mutation status.

suggested_regression_test.assertion: train_and_promote returns learned rules plus a machine-readable inventory proving the held-out fold was excluded and the base kernel checksum did not change.

issues.title: No-scalar and no-host-transfer ranking guarantees are example-side
issues.expected_behavior: XLOG/pyxlog should expose runtime counters or contracts that fail a CUDA nn/4 ranking loop when it materializes score rows, calls scalar extraction, or performs hidden host transfers.
issues.actual_behavior: Resolved in v0.8.9 by pyxlog.runtime_audit.CudaExecutionAudit, a reusable hot-loop audit context that records D2H/H2D counters, score-row downloads, scalar extraction, and fail-closed host materialization violations.
issues.proposed_fix: Implemented a pyxlog CUDA execution audit context that records tensor materialization, scalar extraction, D2H/H2D counters, and score-row downloads for nn/4 calls.

suggested_regression_test.assertion: A CUDA nn/4 ranking session reports zero hot-loop D2H/H2D transfers and fails when score tensors are materialized on host inside the ranking loop.

issues.title: Adapter-boundary and held-out leakage diagnostics are not reusable XLOG features
issues.expected_behavior: XLOG should help declare frozen kernels, adapter-only fact modules, held-out domains, candidate provenance, and leakage checks as reusable diagnostics.
issues.actual_behavior: Resolved in v0.8.9 by xlog_logic::diagnose_module_boundaries, which audits frozen kernel predicates, adapter-only rule violations, held-out module declarations, and held-out-label candidate provenance.
issues.proposed_fix: Implemented XLOG module manifests and diagnostics for read-only kernel modules, adapter-only fact modules, held-out partition declarations, and candidate provenance audits.

suggested_regression_test.assertion: A program with a frozen BFO module and domain adapter modules reports adapter-only boundaries and fails when a held-out adapter mutates the core kernel or uses held-out labels.

issues.title: Robust transfer aggregates require external validator code
issues.expected_behavior: XLOG or pyxlog should provide reusable aggregate diagnostics for leave-one-domain-out macro F1, per-domain minima, confidence intervals, paired tests, and adversarial variants.
issues.actual_behavior: Resolved in v0.8.9 by pyxlog.transfer_diagnostics.compute_transfer_diagnostics, which recomputes grouped macro F1, minimum domain F1, bootstrap confidence intervals, baseline uplift, paired sign tests, and missing group failures from raw records.
issues.proposed_fix: Implemented pyxlog diagnostic helpers for grouped classification metrics, bootstrap confidence intervals, paired significance tests, and required-group coverage checks.

suggested_regression_test.assertion: Given raw prediction records grouped by held-out domain and variant, pyxlog diagnostics recompute macro F1, minimum domain F1, confidence intervals, and missing-domain failures.
